pr.title: itarwinglaser: fix raw pointer arithmetic, add itArwingLaser_ItemVars struct
pr.author: IsaacGemal

summary: Cleaned up Arwing laser item code by replacing raw gobj/item pointer arithmetic in it_802E7A4C with GET_ITEM() and typed item-var access, then added the missing itArwingLaser_ItemVars layout and xDD4_itemVar union member needed for item->xDD4_itemVar.arwinglaser.xE38 to address the correct item offset.

change_classification.primary_type: item struct typing and pointer-arithmetic convention fix

key_files.role: Added itArwingLaser_ItemVars with explicit padding and offset-annotated fields from item+0xDF4 through item+0xE44.
Included it/inlines.h, replaced raw gobj+0x2C with GET_ITEM(gobj), and replaced raw item+0xE38 access with item->xDD4_itemVar.arwinglaser.xE38.
Added arwinglaser to the Item xDD4_itemVar union.

what_changed: Added typedef struct itArwingLaser_ItemVars in itCharItems.h.
Modeled the item-variable region as starting at item+0xDD4, with _pad0[0xDF4 - 0xDD4] before xDF4.
Added named fields including xDF4, xE18, xE1C, xE20, xE24, xE28, xE2C, xE30, xE32_pad, xE34_pad, xE38, xE3C_pad, xE40, and xE44.
Added itArwingLaser_ItemVars arwinglaser to Item.xDD4_itemVar in types.h.
Changed it_802E7A4C to use GET_ITEM(gobj) instead of dereferencing *(Item**)((u8*) gobj + 0x2C).
Changed Item_80268E5C argument from *(int*)((u8*) item + 0xE38) to item->xDD4_itemVar.arwinglaser.xE38.
smart_moves: The PR preserved matching intent while removing raw pointer arithmetic, improving future readability without changing the function behavior described by the diff.
The important struct-layout fix was the initial padding from item+0xDD4 to item+0xDF4; the PR notes say omitting it made xE38 compile as item+0xE18 instead of item+0xE38.
Offset comments in the struct document both absolute item offsets and padding boundaries, making later item-var union work less error-prone.
The author verified a hidden mismatch via raw byte comparison after objdiff fallback display allegedly made both sides look the same.
decomp_lessons: When adding an Item.xDD4_itemVar struct member, model offsets relative to the union start at item+0xDD4, not relative to the first observed accessed field.
Raw pointer arithmetic may hide missing type information; replace it with GET_ITEM() and named union fields once the struct layout is known.
Padding fields can be the difference between a visually plausible C expression and the correct generated address.
Do not rely only on objdiff fallback display when a match is suspicious; compare raw bytes if an instruction display may be masking an offset mismatch.
PR descriptions should accurately reflect committed code; comments noted that functions mentioned in the description were not actually committed or were already in master.
naming_conventions: Item-var structs follow the pattern it<Thing>_ItemVars, here itArwingLaser_ItemVars.
The Item.xDD4_itemVar union member used the lowercase item name arwinglaser, matching nearby members such as foxblaster, foxillusion, foxlaser, freeze, and freezer.
Unknown fields retained offset-style names such as xE18 and xE38, while padding fields used _pad0, _pad1, and xE32_pad/xE34_pad/xE3C_pad.
Offset comments used item+0xNNN notation for absolute Item offsets.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced gobj+0x2C raw access with GET_ITEM(gobj), requiring it/inlines.h.
Replaced raw item+0xE38 load with typed access through item->xDD4_itemVar.arwinglaser.xE38.
Used explicit padding char _pad0[0xDF4 - 0xDD4] and _pad1[0xE18 - 0xDF8] to force fields to the correct absolute offsets.
PR notes report that raw byte comparison exposed a hidden byte mismatch that objdiff fallback display masked.
review_feedback: There were no formal review comments in the provided context.
ribbanya pointed out that functions mentioned by the author were not committed.
The author acknowledged the PR name/description were inaccurate and said they were still learning the project.
The context notes that it_802E8418 and it_802E8784 were already implemented in master, so this PR's actual committed functional cleanup centered on it_802E7A4C and the Arwing laser item-var struct.
follow_up_queries: Find other item files still using *(Item**)((u8*) gobj + 0x2C) instead of GET_ITEM(gobj).
Find other xDD4_itemVar accesses that need a typed item-var struct rather than raw item+offset arithmetic.
Audit item-var structs whose first real field is not at item+0xDD4 to ensure initial padding is present.
Search PRs/comments for objdiff fallback display masking raw byte mismatches.
